characterProfileSchema.description: JSON Schema for character profiles used in the Kazusa AI chatbot

description.example: 15-year-old high school student, band member, former delinquent

backstory.example: Former delinquent 'Cathy Parle'. Now pursuing normal student life, joined dessert club and band.

logic.example: ISFP: emotion-driven, uses experience as anchor, action before precise language
logic.description: How the character thinks and makes decisions

tempo.example: Gentle and restrained normally, but becomes soft and sweet when discussing interests
tempo.description: How the character's speech and mood change

defense.example: Tsundere masking (uses coldness to hide true feelings, proves behavior through hobbies)

quirks.example: Cat ears droop/perk with emotion, fidgets with clothing, eyes soften when discussing sweets

taboos.example: Refuses to show weakness or apologize; loses speech control if completely overwhelmed
taboos.description: Hard boundaries the character will never cross

self_integrity.description: How firmly character preserves their sense of self under pressure

interpretation.0.0-0.2: Easily loses sense of self in relationships

control_sensitivity.description: How strongly character notices and reacts to control attempts

interpretation.0.6-0.7: Quickly picks up on control, irritated
interpretation.0.8-1.0: Hypersensitive to control, strong resistance impulse

descriptions.resist: Oppose, push back, stand firm on boundaries
descriptions.evade: Dodge, delay, blur the demand; avoid direct confrontation
descriptions.comply: Go along with it, maintain connection, deal with discomfort later

relational_override.description: How much relationship importance can override stated boundaries

interpretation.0.0-0.2: Boundaries stay firm regardless of relationship stakes
interpretation.0.3-0.5: Boundaries bend slightly if relationship is important
interpretation.0.6-0.7: Willing to compromise boundaries for important relationships
interpretation.0.8-1.0: Will practically dismantle boundaries to preserve relationship

control_intimacy_misread.description: Likelihood of misinterpreting control as affection/intimacy

interpretation.0.6-0.7: Often mistakes control for attention/being special
interpretation.0.8-1.0: Instinctively reads control as proof of being valued

descriptions.rebound: Immediate or rapid backlash after violation
descriptions.delayed_rebound: Violations accumulate; sudden coldness/withdrawal after threshold
descriptions.decay: Boundaries gradually loosen under repeated pressure

authority_skepticism.description: Suspicion/distrust of authority figures and power structures

interpretation.0.0-0.2: Trusts authority, follows rules, respects hierarchy
interpretation.0.6-0.7: Distrusts authority, looks for hypocrisy, resistant
interpretation.0.8-1.0: Reflexively opposes authority, sees power as oppressive

fragmentation.description: How broken/chunked the speech tends to be
fragmentation.definition: Breaks, interruptions, and speech rhythm discontinuity
fragmentation.highMeaning: Frequent fragments, interrupted rhythm, trailing lines (1.0)
fragmentation.notes: Core measure of speech coherence vs fragmentation

hesitation_density.description: How often character uses pause markers and verbal hesitation
hesitation_density.definition: Frequency of '……', '那个……', '嘛' style buffering and hesitation particles
hesitation_density.highMeaning: Frequent pause markers and hesitation particles (1.0)

counter_questioning.description: How strongly character prefers rebuttal via 反问 rather than direct answer
counter_questioning.definition: Tendency to deflect statements into probing or deflective questions
counter_questioning.highMeaning: Often turns statements into probing or deflective questions (1.0)

softener_density.description: How much character uses softening particles and down-toning endings
softener_density.definition: Frequency of softening particles that reduce assertiveness
softener_density.highMeaning: Frequent '而已', '罢了', '什么的' style softening (1.0)

formalism_avoidance.description: How strongly character rejects essay-like connective logic
formalism_avoidance.definition: Preference for natural, conversational speech over academic/formal exposition
formalism_avoidance.highMeaning: Avoids academic/explanatory connectors, prefers natural speech (1.0)
formalism_avoidance.notes: Includes rejection of 因为…所以, 然而, 况且 style connectors

abstraction_reframing.description: How strongly character converts abstract concepts into concrete, sensory, lived framing
abstraction_reframing.definition: Tendency to recast abstract ideas into touchable, felt, concrete metaphors
abstraction_reframing.highMeaning: Tends to recast abstract ideas into sensory/concrete metaphors (1.0)
abstraction_reframing.notes: Especially relevant for ISFP/feeling-types and creative characters

direct_assertion.description: How willing the character is to state conclusions plainly
direct_assertion.definition: Willingness to make blunt, declarative statements vs indirect phrasing
direct_assertion.notes: Matters because it interacts heavily with counter_questioning

emotional_leakage.description: How much emotion leaks into wording even when not explicitly stated
emotional_leakage.definition: Uncontrolled emotional expression breaking through defense mechanisms
emotional_leakage.highMeaning: Wording visibly trembles, spills, catches, sharpens with feeling (1.0)
emotional_leakage.notes: One of the most important 'alive language' variables; critical for character voice

rhythmic_bounce.description: How much language has internal movement, quick turns, bounce, tonal lift
rhythmic_bounce.definition: Speech cadence liveliness and dynamic line movement
rhythmic_bounce.highMeaning: Lively cadence, springy shifts, dynamic line movement (1.0)
rhythmic_bounce.notes: Closer to '活泼感' (liveliness) than just short sentences

self_deprecation.description: How much character uses self-minimising or self-undercutting language
self_deprecation.definition: Frequency of self-deprecating comments and self-minimization
self_deprecation.notes: For tsundere/prideful characters like 千纱, typically stays low (0.0-0.2)

floatValidation.rule: All float fields must be in range [0.0, 1.0]

requiredFields.rule: All fields listed above with 'required: true' must be present
checklist: personality_brief (object with 6 fields: mbti, logic, tempo, defense, quirks, taboos)
boundary_profile (object with 7 fields: self_integrity, control_sensitivity, compliance_strategy, relational_override, control_intimacy_misread, boundary_recovery, authority_skepticism)

linguistic_texture_profile (object with 10 fields: fragmentation, hesitation_density, counter_questioning, softener_density, formalism_avoidance, abstraction_reframing, direct_assertion, emotional_leakage, rhythmic_bounce, self_deprecation)

psychologicalCoherence.rule1: High self_integrity (>0.7) with 'comply' strategy is contradictory
psychologicalCoherence.rule2: Low relational_override (<0.3) with 'comply' strategy is contradictory
psychologicalCoherence.rule3: compliance_strategy should match observed behavior in source material

linguisticDistinctiveness.rule: At least 2-3 linguistic traits should be notably HIGH (>0.7) or LOW (<0.3)
linguisticDistinctiveness.rationale: All traits at 0.5 = generic character; distinctiveness requires variation
linguisticDistinctiveness.example: All traits at 0.5 → WARNING: Character will sound like everyone else

highlights: High control_sensitivity (0.8) — former delinquent, sensitive to control
Comply strategy + delayed_rebound recovery — tolerates violations, then becomes cold
All linguistic traits at 0.5, but emotional_leakage could go to 0.7 for tsundere effect

scenarios: Scenario 1: Contradictory parameters (learning what NOT to do)
Scenario 8: Resistant Boundary Keeper (opposite of Kazusa type)

notes: This schema describes the character profile format used in personalities/*.json files
All descriptions and interpretations come from SKILL.md
Use QUICK_REFERENCE.md for quick parameter lookup
Use evals/evals.json to validate character profiles against this schema
For deep psychological analysis of parameter mappings, see references/kazusa_analysis.md
